[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zlDPxDHCSe8GlVxjXZtZRt8RmMiHH_rmhmb_QXuKOypDq1d9p9hdBUhukqOKX9gZcbXRw8qc1mA0xbvB92gu-fc86Fx0uBL7CVnaKZoCGwUxW9rRI0pawCLSZ-S1-Hxi2NjmjHOlWqZ93hacq2Ajix1BgrxXLIlx7swJljDTOisfaJW6plnIgLOQPV-gjTrajn9jwNsBJLcnGlFwD8P5loescmH2BOyALcaw7kzmP2Fxd_CyyS3vD0PZZuE8ATVONhXmwDKSx54_1DtI9Vm_weA3qhwJiIVuFxX0eLKa0QCSEitGphu93GpHH9gLSRVK9XB50lN9sF9MZgamjD7Swt2necqg2agbNXihWgQWezDaNH1LVcEr7wrSyAfTBMjeab8C_Z__prwTfFZ0lSoTzB/3f3/eTz65YzLQV2pjNq_FuUfGQ/h1/8lVsMMiplp80UvxBy2vP8ioXqcZR57CNvjbsS0MfXds) These statistics show that Nevada represents the best of what our country has to offer. Folks, the Census numbers also tell a story about what it means to be a Nevadan. **Being a Nevadan means enjoying equal opportunities and taking immense pride in our diversity.** Make no mistake: these two values sit at the center of our fight for _expanding health care access, increasing education opportunities, leading the way on climate change, and so much more._
**[But, to be frank with you, I cannot do this without your continued support. clean_body: Check out the Census results | | | | | Folks, I’ve always said that Nevada is great because of the people that live here. I feel that more strongly than ever after taking a look at the recent Census results from the past decade. These statistics show that Nevada represents the best of what our country has to offer. And as we grow in size and diversity, I’ll need your help to make sure that my campaign can grow its organizing program to meet the moment. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Folks, the Census numbers also tell a story about what it means to be a Nevadan. Being a Nevadan means enjoying equal opportunities and taking immense pride in our diversity. Make no mistake: these two values sit at the center of our fight for expanding health care access, increasing education opportunities, leading the way on climate change, and so much more. | | | | | | | | | | | | With your help, I’m positive that we’ll keep our state on its steady upward trajectory. Thanks a bunch, Steve Sisolak | | If you'd like to support Steve Sisolak for Governor, you can | Paid for by Steve Sisolak for Governor Steve Sisolak for Governor P.O. Box 850 Las Vegas, NV 89101 mailto:info@stevesisolak.com | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Sisolak for Nevada, please .

name: Dave Giles for Congress
subject: Happy 2021 Labor Day
body:
Hello Peter! Labor Day is dedicated to the social and economic achievements of American workers. It
| | |
---
| [ ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/261510?id=3706572.4250.1.63da552b4984b01ac24b990313efe0c5)
---
**__Hello Peter!** Labor Day is dedicated to the social and economic achievements of American workers. It constitutes a yearly national tribute to the contributions workers have made to the strength, prosperity, and well-being of our country. Some records show that Peter J. McGuire, general secretary of the Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners and a co-founder of the American Federation of Labor, was first in suggesting a day to honor those "who from rude nature have delved and carved all the grandeur we behold." The first Labor Day holiday was celebrated on Tuesday, September 5, 1882, in New York City, in accordance with the plans of the Central Labor Union. The Central Labor Union held its second Labor Day holiday just a year later, on September 5, 1883.
# **Happy Labor Day**
---
| | [ Support Dave ](https://sable.godaddy.com/c/261510?id=3706572.4251.1.21165df97d0265e891520544e49ca067)
---
**Thank you!**
---
Sincerely,
---
|

|

**Dave Giles**
Candidate
United States Congress
Arizona's 9th District

name: Terry McAuliffe
subject: Sorry to bother you, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Terry for Virginia
body:
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35297467/304625577/-2023814932?refcode=20210906.1_TM_9AF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTg2OGEyMi0wZTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cb79IZ4n8DimtksSEkq3RBg--SNVgB6Qm8sr4ssRvZ8=)

| | Derek,

I’m sorry to bother you over a long weekend, but it’s important. We’re just two weeks from the start of early voting and a recent poll from a top-rated 538 pollster shows me _**tied with Trump-endorsed Glenn Youngkin.**_

Our fundraising has completely stalled out over the last couple days and I need your help so we can kick it into high gear before Virginians start voting on September 17th.

_**[Please contribute anything you can afford right now. Your contribution goes directly toward our efforts to turn out voters, defend Virginia’s progress, and stop Donald Trump.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35297468/304625578/-2023814932?refcode=20210906.1_TM_9AF&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTg2OGEyMi0wZTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cb79IZ4n8DimtksSEkq3RBg--SNVgB6Qm8sr4ssRvZ8=)**_

If you’re like me, you’re getting a lot of fundraising emails right now. I don’t doubt the urgency of those races, but let me tell you why no race is more important than Virginia in 2021.

For the last decade, Virginia Democrats have led the nation by delivering victory after victory for working families.

Right now all of that progress is under attack. If my opponent Glenn Youngkin becomes Virginia’s governor, he’ll roll back every inch we fought so hard for.

Let’s be clear, Glenn Youngkin is a right-wing Trump acolyte. He wants to ban abortion, he pushes Trump’s big lie, and if he becomes Governor, he’ll put more guns on our streets.

Moreover, Donald Trump has endorsed Youngkin three separate times. If Youngkin wins, Trump’s 2024 presidential run will get a huge momentum boost heading into 2022.

clean_body: | | | = | | Derek, I’m sorry to bother you over a long weekend, but it’s important. We’re just two weeks from the start of early voting and a recent poll from a top-rated 538 pollster shows me tied with Trump-endorsed Glenn Youngkin. Our fundraising has completely stalled out over the last couple days and I need your help so we can kick it into high gear before Virginians start voting on September 17th. = If you’re like me, you’re getting a lot of fundraising emails right now. I don’t doubt the urgency of those races, but let me tell you why no race is more important than Virginia in 2021. For the last decade, Virginia Democrats have led the nation by delivering victory after victory for working families. Right now all of that progress is under attack. If my opponent Glenn Youngkin becomes Virginia’s governor, he’ll roll back every inch we fought so hard for. Let’s be clear, Glenn Youngkin is a right-wing Trump acolyte. He wants to ban abortion, he pushes Trump’s big lie, and if he becomes Governor, he’ll put more guns on our streets. Moreover, Donald Trump has endorsed Youngkin three separate times. If Youngkin wins, Trump’s 2024 presidential run will get a huge momentum boost heading into 2022. = | | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | = Thanks for your time, Terry =** Terry McAuliffe for Virginia PO Box 26112 Arlington, VA 22215 If you would like to opt-out of future emails, click. Happy Labor Day, Peter!

Today, we honor and recognize the American labor movement including the contributions and achievements that workers have accomplished in our country.

**Ensuring that there is dignity in ALL labor, including campaign labor, is a cause that’s close to our hearts here at Barrier To Entry PAC.**

That’s why our sister organization and subsidiary movement, Pay Your Interns PAC, is specifically focused on upholding ethical labor practices, fighting for workers' rights, and promoting equitable access to employment opportunities.

Together, we must all continue fighting for these labor values by eliminating unpaid internships, demanding a minimum wage of $15 per hour, providing adequate benefits, and supporting our unions.

**Let today serve as a reminder of the importance of employee welfare.** Thanks for being in this fight to lift up all workers! **_~ Barrier To Entry PAC & Pay Your Interns PAC_** [**P.S. Thanks for being in this fight to lift up all workers! ~ Barrier To Entry PAC & Pay Your Interns PAC = ********** ********** ****************EXPRESS DONATE to Barrier to Entry PAC**************** ****************If you've stored payment info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:**************** | ****************= "="**************** | ****************= "="**************** | ****************= "="**************** | ****************= "="**************** ---|---|---|--- BARRIER TO ENTRY PAC supports candidates and campaign staff from working class backgrounds as they carve their paths in the Democratic political space. We provide direct cash assistance for essential items like phones, cars, laptops, and living stipends -- which often times, these candidates and their campaigns don't have access to. We also serve as a resource for individual mentorship and training to ensure candidates and their staff feel supported and equipped to enter the political space. Pitch in today to help provide necessary support and resources for working class candidates today! >> __= | || || Barrier To Entry PAC PO Box 6623 Cleveland, OH 44101 United States | |

name: Adam Schiff
subject: humbly asking
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Schiff for Congress
body:
|
#
Derek, I wanted to thank you for joining our campaign at such a critical time — for our planet, for our economy, and for our democracy itself. Every grassroots supporter who makes their voice heard in elections, holds elected officials accountable, and refuses to lose faith in our democratic process means so much to me. It often feels like the GOP is throwing a new attack at us every single day, but it’s your resilience and passion that keeps me fighting for a better future for all of us. I’m working every day to fulfill the big, bold, progressive mandate that voters elected us to pass and fight back against the authoritarianism that has taken hold of Donald Trump’s Republican Party. Together, we’ve restored decency to the White House and flipped the Senate. We passed the American Rescue Plan, providing millions of Americans with relief from the COVID-19 pandemic and an expanded Child Tax Credit that will cut the child poverty rate in _half_ this year. But there’s still more progress to be made. We need to reverse the climate crisis. Make health care universal and bring down prescription drug prices. Defend voting rights. Pass student debt relief. Reform our criminal justice system. Invest in our economy and its people. **And above all else protect our democracy.** In order to do that, we must abolish the filibuster, and I’m applying all of the pressure that I can for the Senate to do just that. But we also must defend and expand our narrow Democratic majorities. This is where your support makes all the difference: **[, DerekI’m humbly asking — can you chip in $10 or any amount you can to give us the resources to fight for progress, protect our Democratic majorities, and uphold our democracy?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35299479/304633945/-840992817?refcode=em-welcome-eo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODYxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmQ1ZDIxZC0xNDBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u6iQk6HEjLCy4flSqMcwXtuCFp_GwQ8Uqza0FkW0XiI=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35299481/304633947/-840992817?email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=em-welcome-eo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODYxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MmQ1ZDIxZC0xNDBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=u6iQk6HEjLCy4flSqMcwXtuCFp_GwQ8Uqza0FkW0XiI=)

name: Conor Lamb
subject: This Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CONOR LAMB FOR SENATE Conor Lamb for Senate
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35303342/304648737/-1576960734?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzE3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzc5ZTRiNi0xYTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oXFDGN5bJ5xcQjpxc92IIVF6t_jc89IcSOaVHaWveZU=)

Friend,

On this Labor Day, and every day, we honor the achievements of those in the workforce who have helped us secure fair wages, hours, working conditions, and treatment by employers.

All workers deserve a living wage for doing a day's work. But for nearly half of our labor force, that just isn't the case. I'm working to show our allies in organized labor that they can count on me to fight for their rights, keep my word, and always put working people first.

Earlier this year the PRO Act passed in the house, **a true game changer**. This bill represents an opportunity for us to rally together and finally begin to correct the wage and health inequalities that hard working families have to deal with every day. But with Democrats holding on by _just the slightest_ margin, we cannot represent our workforce and repair our country's crumbling infrastructure without more support in the Senate.

This Labor Day, we must do more than offer platitudes about the dignity of work.

**[We must send a message: We're in the fight for fair and livable wages. We are in the fight for safe workplaces. We are in the fight for a secure retirement. And until that is a reality, the fight does not end. Sign your name to help move this bill forward.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35303343/304648738/1487641941?ms=em21_210906_proact&refcode=em21_210906_proact&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzE3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzc5ZTRiNi0xYTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oXFDGN5bJ5xcQjpxc92IIVF6t_jc89IcSOaVHaWveZU=&emci=4c32205d-eb0c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=0c79e4b6-1a0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18516396)**

I will always stand in solidarity with our workers as they organize, bargain, and demand better.

**I'm truly thankful for the continued support from labor that our campaign has received. We need to stand with labor now more than ever as we look to get our economy back on track and continue to recover from COVID-19.**

**[So what do you say, friends? Sign the pledge: On Labor Day and every day, we stand in solidarity with all of our brothers and sisters in labor who demand better.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35303344/304648739/1487641941?ms=em21_210906_proact&refcode=em21_210906_proact&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzE3OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzc5ZTRiNi0xYTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oXFDGN5bJ5xcQjpxc92IIVF6t_jc89IcSOaVHaWveZU=&emci=4c32205d-eb0c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=0c79e4b6-1a0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18516396)**

All workers deserve a living wage for doing a day's work. But for nearly half of our labor force, that just isn't the case. I'm working to show our allies in organized labor that they can count on me to fight for their rights, keep my word, and always put working people first. name: Never Again PAC
subject: Fascism and organized labor can't co-exist
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NEVER AGAIN PAC
body:
Today we honor the accomplishments of the hard-working men and women who fought tirelessly to improve working conditions for people all across this country. It's because of their efforts that we get to enjoy a 40-hour work week, no child labor, safe working conditions, overtime pay, Social Security, and anti-discrimination laws. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/35303473/304648885/-2023352048?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBhNzllNGI2LTFhMGYtZWMxMS05ODFmLTUwMWFjNTdiYTNlZCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=fll9wiRroZPoRnndFMoZHoOYhzs_kUN85WiEI3qaS1M=&emci=ed2547d8-d80c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=0a79e4b6-1a0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=16335001) Happy Labor Day, team.

Today we honor the accomplishments of the hard-working men and women who fought tirelessly to improve working conditions for people all across this country. It's because of their efforts that we get to enjoy a 40-hour work week, no child labor, safe working conditions, overtime pay, Social Security, and anti-discrimination laws.

While it might not seem like it, labor unions and democracy go hand in hand.

FDR once said, **"We have come to a clear realization of the fact that true individual freedom cannot exist without economic security and independence.”** Labor unions have fought for decades to provide individuals with the economic security they deserve. Their work has helped both mold and bolster our nation's democracy.

But the fascism we're seeing from many of our elected officials today is troubling for the future of organized labor.

Fascism and organized labor can't co-exist, so it's no surprise why Republicans in the Treason Caucus are trying to block pro-worker legislation. Earlier this year, Democrats in the House passed the PRO Act -- a bill that would provide protections for workers trying to organize. This bill is labor unions' biggest legislative priority this year, **but only five Republicans voted to pass it.**

Republicans in Congress are continuing to prove they're not on the side of workers. So long as they remain in office, organized labor and our democracy remain at risk.

This Labor Day, we hope you join us in taking time to honor the achievements of the labor movement and recommitting to defending workers' rights.

Thanks,

Never Again PAC

| PAID FOR BY NEVER AGAIN PAC

name: Jenna Henry, Keystone Progress
subject: Supporting workers since the 1700s.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=090621 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=090621]

Dear Friend,

Pennsylvania has always been on the cutting edge of labor movements.

We were some of the first in America to organize when printers in Philadelphia held the first ever recorded strike for higher wages in 1776. Our state has continued to lead in forming unions, workers' cooperatives, federations, and benevolent organizations. We have rallied to improve working conditions and to create stability in our homes by demanding fairer wages.

True to those who came before us who were determined to make this a better state, we are continuing to fight threats to our workers and struggling to ensure that they are safe and well-compensated. We work with unions and other labor organizations to make this a more progressive state -- which, at its roots, we know it has always been.

On this Labor Day, which you hopefully have off, please consider signing up for a training session [https://secure.everyaction.com/x6XWMbCFvU2fWByvZDXyAg2?contactdata=T672pkzxYGdcxaW9QdUoUftZgHl47wSXQrT%2FC3xue0pMIymK2z0ApoUCjKtAZsp2fu2jYt4o82Y13mnNhH2GOy311cfNfAj9kjFMAVi3ooimXxDjE5%2FNO6RMHB9L4vPMPHkJXTqsWA1Wp%2BdTxgjy3w%3D%3D] or to volunteer [https://secure.everyaction.com/uzGCPSTbqE-PPaK8Nmgqjw2?contactdata=T672pkzxYGdcxaW9QdUoUftZgHl47wSXQrT%2FC3xue0pMIymK2z0ApoUCjKtAZsp2fu2jYt4o82Y13mnNhH2GOy311cfNfAj9kjFMAVi3ooimXxDjE5%2FNO6RMHB9L4vPMPHkJXTqsWA1Wp%2BdTxgjy3w%3D%3D] to help create a more just and equitable Pennsylvania for all of our workers.

We hope you have a relaxing Labor Day!

Jenna Henry
Keystone Progress

https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/ [https://www.facebook.com/KPEdFund/]

| | Happy Labor Day, folks! Today we honor all hard-working Americans and the labor movement that continues to fight for safe working conditions, fair hours, and living wages. Labor Day was recognized as a federal holiday in 1894, and we’ve certainly come a long way since then. **But, we can still do better for hard-working American families.** For starters, we need to increase the federal minimum wage to that of a _living_ wage. Virginia is in the process of gradually raising the minimum wage from $7.25 to $15 by 2026. In 2022, the Virginia state minimum wage will be $11 an hour. Elaine’s fighting for a livable minimum wage for all Americans at the federal level and a permanent expanded Child Tax Credit, which will pull millions of Americans out of poverty. She knows that no one should have to work multiple jobs just to make ends meet or provide for their families. So, today we’ll celebrate with barbeques and cookouts, and tomorrow we’ll get back to work fighting for the rights of workers across the country. \-- Team Elaine | | | [Donate »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310493/304662116/-1705427475?refcode=EM_BS_LUR_210906_FR_Laborday_A0_na_U1_X1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDA3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDgwZjcxYi0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8Fxin1pAX6G8Piwt3pzacDQrJQRJCW-S7xpLQmE9Qx4=)

name: John Cranley
subject: This Labor Day, I stand with Ohio’s workers and unions.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the John Cranley Committee
body:
| Friend, On Labor Day, we recognize and honor the contributions of the union members and labor activists whose continued fight for workers’ rights has led to where we are today. **A 40-hour work week. Health benefits. The minimum wage._All that and more_ is thanks to organized labor.** From factory-line workers to first responders, union workers are the backbone of our communities, and I will always stand with them. Friend, unions built the middle class, and as we look to rebuild from the COVID-19 pandemic and revive Ohio’s economy, creating good-paying, union jobs will be key. In Cincinnati, we’ve stood with our union brothers and sisters by passing Ohio’s only municipal prevailing wage law, implementing new workplace safety measures, creating a $15 minimum wage for city workers, and giving our union employees their first raise in years. **We have walked the walk when it comes to supporting workers and growing the middle class.** And, we’re going to do the same for Ohio. My plan will rebuild Ohio’s middle class by creating 30,000 jobs per year that pay a minimum of $60,000 per year to build vital infrastructure that Ohio needs to offer middle-class opportunities to all. These jobs will include advanced manufacturing, building high-speed broadband, and investing in cleaner energy, cleaner water, and safer roads and bridges. Private sector growth requires public infrastructure like good roads, high-speed internet and WiFi, clean water, access to affordable clean energy, and quality education and workforce training. My comeback plan will lead to job growth and opportunities for better wages for all. If you elect me governor, I will do everything in my power to defend the right of workers to organize, because the struggle for workers’ rights is never over. **Today, we reaffirm our commitment to continuing the fight.** I hope you are having a safe Labor Day weekend, John
**[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310502/304662127/-1406512299?refcode=em_JC_OTH_LaborDay_090621_eb&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZjgwZjcxYi0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ygeVyRmWniA_hqpbWDOEeQ3kOtyWGlTcyShH6TZnZMU=)**
---
John Cranley has spent his career working towards creating a fairer, more just world. It's why he founded Ohio’s Innocence Project back in 2003, why he ran for mayor of Cincinnati — the town where he grew up – and why he and his wife, Dena are raising their son to respect the principles of justice, equality, and compassion. **Now, he's running for governor. He's already led a comeback for Cincinnati, and he's ready to lead a comeback for Ohio, too.**

clean_body: | Friend, On Labor Day, we recognize and honor the contributions of the union members and labor activists whose continued fight for workers’ rights has led to where we are today. A 40-hour work week. Health benefits. The minimum wage.All that and more is thanks to organized labor. From factory-line workers to first responders, union workers are the backbone of our communities, and I will always stand with them. Friend, unions built the middle class, and as we look to rebuild from the COVID-19 pandemic and revive Ohio’s economy, creating good-paying, union jobs will be key. In Cincinnati, we’ve stood with our union brothers and sisters by passing Ohio’s only municipal prevailing wage law, implementing new workplace safety measures, creating a $15 minimum wage for city workers, and giving our union employees their first raise in years. We have walked the walk when it comes to supporting workers and growing the middle class. And, we’re going to do the same for Ohio. My plan will rebuild Ohio’s middle class by creating 30,000 jobs per year that pay a minimum of $60,000 per year to build vital infrastructure that Ohio needs to offer middle-class opportunities to all. These jobs will include advanced manufacturing, building high-speed broadband, and investing in cleaner energy, cleaner water, and safer roads and bridges. Private sector growth requires public infrastructure like good roads, high-speed internet and WiFi, clean water, access to affordable clean energy, and quality education and workforce training. My comeback plan will lead to job growth and opportunities for better wages for all. If you elect me governor, I will do everything in my power to defend the right of workers to organize, because the struggle for workers’ rights is never over. Today, we reaffirm our commitment to continuing the fight. I hope you are having a safe Labor Day weekend, John = John Cranley has spent his career working towards creating a fairer, more just world. It's why he founded Ohio’s Innocence Project back in 2003, why he ran for mayor of Cincinnati — the town where he grew up – and why he and his wife, Dena are raising their son to respect the principles of justice, equality, and compassion. Now, he's running for governor. He's already led a comeback for Cincinnati, and he's ready to lead a comeback for Ohio, too. = Paid for by the John Cranley Committee JOHN CRANLEY COMMITTEE 30 GARFIELD PLACE SUITE 900 CINCINNATI, OH 45202 United States Click here to

On January 6th, 2021, violent insurrectionists stormed the U.S. Capitol, putting a bedrock principle of our democracy -- the peaceful transfer of power -- at risk. They were inspired by Donald Trump’s lies and egged on by extremists like Ted Cruz.

Despite the tragic outcome, Republicans keep doubling down on Trump’s anti-democratic lies.

name: Malcolm Kenyatta
subject: Honoring the power of working people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Malcolm PAC
body:
#

Today we honor the power of working people — the power of what we can accomplish when we unite to fight for an economy that works for all of us, not just the wealthy few.
Unions built the middle-class by giving workers the power of banding together and advocating for fair wages, better benefits, and safe working conditions.
But that's all under attack. Greedy corporate giants like Amazon are making out like bandits while hardworking families suffer.
**So it's time for Congress to step up. We cannot continue to let working families get left behind.**
[Will you join me in urging the U.S. Senate to pass the Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act? We need to show this bill has overwhelming support, especially from folks in Pennsylvania, so I hope you'll sign my petition >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310967/304663585/-18859834?refcode=em210906-1-full&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzQ0ZjIyMS0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-MbOcXgzFlik_KjV7-iawqYrvfuKrqxGGCVtieEx94=&emci=eb06aebd-850d-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=8c44f221-230f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18034979)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310969/304663590/-18859834?refcode=em210906-1-full&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzQ0ZjIyMS0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-MbOcXgzFlik_KjV7-iawqYrvfuKrqxGGCVtieEx94=&emci=eb06aebd-850d-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=8c44f221-230f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18034979)**
Thanks to the power of collective bargaining, working people in this country have had a pathway to financial stability for over a century.
But with employers' _(and state governments')_ increasing hostility toward them, the number of unionized workers is declining.
In 1980, nearly a third of all workers in Pennsylvania were members of a union. But today, union membership has fallen to less than 15%. Congress must act to reverse that trend and put us on a path to rebuilding a thriving middle class.
Since the beginning of the pandemic, working families have had to confront unprecedented challenges.
Ensuring workers' rights to advocate for better wages and safe working conditions is the only way for us to build a robust economic recovery in these uncertain times.
The PRO Act will help accomplish that by updating outdated national labor laws to strengthen collective bargaining efforts and expand protections for more workers.
It passed with bipartisan support in the House, and it's time for the Senate to send it to President Biden's desk.
**We applauded the workers who built this country and got us through the pandemic. Now it’s time for us to do right by them.**
[If you agree, will you join me today in speaking up for working families and urge the Senate to pass the PRO Act?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310970/304663591/-18859834?refcode=em210906-1-full&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzQ0ZjIyMS0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-MbOcXgzFlik_KjV7-iawqYrvfuKrqxGGCVtieEx94=&emci=eb06aebd-850d-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=8c44f221-230f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18034979)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35310972/304663593/-18859834?refcode=em210906-1-full&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODc1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzQ0ZjIyMS0yMzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-MbOcXgzFlik_KjV7-iawqYrvfuKrqxGGCVtieEx94=&emci=eb06aebd-850d-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=8c44f221-230f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=18034979)**
In solidarity,
Malcolm

name: The Fairness Project
subject: Sign our pledge to fight for working people in America >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Fairness Project
body:
The Fairness Project was born out of the labor movement, and we’re proud to be carrying the legacy of union organizers as we fight for higher wages, better benefits, and economic fairness.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/35311961/304668309/-415323875?refcode=E11127-20210906-LaborDayEspy-header-Holiday-RegTemplate-WorkerRightsMinWage-NA&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Yjk0ZjIyZC0yNTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XeQIbjZD16_nUpowybargkYHC6zA3vtGeED6jhdeK-U=) Hi team — and happy Labor Day! We’ve won 20 of the 21 ballot campaigns we’ve worked on. We’ve delivered health care for over 800,000 people across the country by expanding Medicaid and we’ve helped put over $19 billion into the pockets of workers by raising the minimum wage. This work is only possible because of the generations of labor leaders who established the minimum wage in the first place and continue to fight to make health care a human right. **This Labor Day, we’re celebrating all of the hard-working individuals who have fought with us to get ballot measures passed to improve the quality of life for working people in America. ** But there’s lots more to do — right now we’re planning more campaigns for 2021 and 2022. **If you’re with us in the fight for progressive change for working people in America, will you sign our pledge today?[_Sign on to stand with us today!_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/35311962/304668310/-1180759046?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Yjk0ZjIyZC0yNTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XeQIbjZD16_nUpowybargkYHC6zA3vtGeED6jhdeK-U=&emci=6cbc9f07-360c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=4b94f22d-250f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=561582)** | **[STAND WITH TFP →](https://click.everyaction.com/k/35311963/304668311/-1180759046?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Yjk0ZjIyZC0yNTBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XeQIbjZD16_nUpowybargkYHC6zA3vtGeED6jhdeK-U=&emci=6cbc9f07-360c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=4b94f22d-250f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=561582)**

name: Alex Lasry
subject: Let’s celebrate Wisconsin's unions today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lasry for Wisconsin **[alexlasry
body:
Paying workers family-sustaining wages, providing them with good benefits, and treating everyone with the dignity and respect they deserve doesn’t have to be controversial. | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35312295/304669528/112948214?refcode=EM_C_210906LD_N_AL_90DA_4_NG_N_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODcxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZWY1ODY5My0yNjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GGnkBKlmN8qHHOa9wIrZJaUBQNCD1yN58hH-hO9CJCg=)
---
Peter, today is Labor Day -- a day to celebrate our union workers and recognize their hard-fought victories for all of us.

When unions win, we all win. Their victories over the centuries have benefitted us all, and I’m proud to always stand with unions and to have IATSE Local 18, IBEW Local 494, and Teamsters Joint Council 39 backing our campaign to oust Ron Johnson.

I believe that progressive values, and union values, are good for business. Paying workers family-sustaining wages, providing them with good benefits, and treating everyone with the dignity and respect they deserve doesn’t have to be controversial. Working with the Bucks, we’ve shown that it’s possible to do all of this while still running a successful and profitable business.

If we work together, I can and will join our unions to ensure that Wisconsin’s hardworking men and women continue to have safe workplaces -- where our labor laws are respected and enforced -- and to work aggressively to support laws that assist workers and their families like raising the minimum wage.

Happy Labor Day,

Alex
---
**** | **** | We need to re-think how our economy works, and we need new voices in Washington. Alex Lasry has a fresh perspective and record of actually getting things done. We can beat Ron Johnson by building a grassroots coalition, but it will take all of us. name: John Fetterman
subject: I can't think of a better way to celebrate Labor Day than this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
My commitment to the union way of life is absolute — even if it puts me at odds with my own party, and no matter how it polls.
#
Peter,
Before we get too far into today's day off, I am asking you to [** _please sign my petition to honor the labor unions and workers who build this country by urgently passing the PRO Act._**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=1&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)__
Because on Labor Day and every day, protecting the Union Way of Life is the right call.
And just think about it. Weekends, the eight hour workday, benefits: All thanks to unions and the centuries-long labor movement that built this nation.
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=2&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)
Now, in the face of unprecedented attacks on workers and the right to form a union, we must pass the PRO Act — a crucial piece of legislation to put power back into the hands of workers, fight for our union brothers and sisters, and expand the union way of life for all workers across America. **The House passed this bill a whopping 181 days ago — but the Senate STILL has yet to follow suit and send the PRO Act to President Biden's desk.**
I can't think of a better way to celebrate Labor Day today than to actually pass crucial protections for millions of American workers and their families. ** _[Will you add your name to my petition to tell the Senate to pass the PRO Act immediately?](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=3&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)_**
**[Pass the PRO Act →](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=4&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)**
My commitment to the union way of life is absolute — even if it puts me at odds with my own party, and no matter how it polls. I am proud to stand with the Steelworkers, the United Food and Commercial Workers, the Building Trades, and all of the union workers in Pennsylvania, even when others turn their backs on them. I always have and I always will.
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=5&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)
If I were in the Senate representing Pennsylvania right now, you can bet that I'd be a strong YES vote for the PRO Act. **But working families can't afford to wait any longer. So I'm asking you to join me this Labor Day:****_[Tell the Senate to pass the PRO Act. It's time to put power into the hands of workers and expand the union way of life.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=6&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)_**
**[Pass the PRO Act →](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=7&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)**
Thank you for signing on — and enjoy your Labor Day. ✊
John
John Fetterman
Lieutenant Governor of Pennsylvania
P.S. Check out this AMAZING sign we saw displayed outside the Steelworkers local in Bethlehem, PA. Pretty incredible stuff. Be sure to thank a worker today, and then [** _add your name next to mine to tell the Senate to pass the PRO Act._**](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=8&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)
[](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/pass_the_pro_act?source=em20210906-2535&t=9&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)
**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails-footer-test?refcode=em20210906-2535-footer-test-ctrl&t=14&refcode2=2535_434096_3dVZjN&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/JohnFettermanPA/) | [](https://twitter.com/johnfetterman) | [](https://www.instagram.com/johnfetterman/)
Email us: info@johnfetterman.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Fetterman for PA, PO Box 6061, Pittsburgh PA 15211.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.johnfetterman.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/personal_info/?t=15&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.johnfetterman.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=16&akid=2535%2E434096%2E3dVZjN)

Paid for by Fetterman for PA.

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Solidarity with workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Peter,

Happy Labor Day! Many folks have the day off from work and school today, but it is important to remember that today is about so much more than that. Labor Day is an opportunity to say **thank you** to the Labor movement and all who fought hard for the eight-hour workday, weekends, overtime, safer workplaces, Social Security, Medicare, and so much more.

We must also pledge to have workers' backs. The past year and a half have reminded us just how essential our **essential** workers are -- and how critical workplace protections are for the health and safety of our workers. Without them, we couldn't have kept our hospitals, grocery stores, sanitation facilities, and so many other services up and running.

With that in mind, we must fight for working people. I'm proud to be a co-sponsor of the Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act, and I'm fighting in Congress for a higher national minimum wage, paid family leave, stronger support for those in the care economy, and expanded affordable health care access.

I'm also proud to show solidarity with the Washington State Labor Council and all workers - whether they work in homecare or childcare, doing construction or building airplanes, at the port, a state agency, the shipyard, or the post office, in a classroom, a grocery store, or someplace else. We should have the backs of workers.

**I 'm not done fighting for an economy that works for all of us and protects the rights of our workers. This Labor Day, I hope you'll join me in committing to this fight.**

It's an honor to represent you,

Derek

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/n2mVGouMYcJE0psF_SPBRoWVe1CLxMicp5I1uzyvr98=/3GglpXUNKKEBp-iXKQ5AluM0VrbeHMNLaCsbG5Hfh8k=) | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/n2mVGouMYcJE0psF_SPBRoWVe1CLxMicp5I1uzyvr98=/HnB6wNWyYIpFwo8yur2Lxh0_YFbTu9BGAWO4kMz5RPU=)

name: Derek Marshall
subject: We must protect our unions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress
body:
**Today is Labor Day. **I believe Labor Day is a time to remember that for hundreds of years unions have led the fight for equal rights and economic and social justice. Unions and workers risked life and limb to give us the 8-hour workday, weekends and fair wages. **Today, we honor their sacrifice and remember their power. **

I understand the importance of unions. **I myself was a UFCW Local 400 Union member.** They made sure I had a fair wage and protections in my job. That’s why I will always stand by our unions and fight to protect the right to unionize.

**When I am elected to Congress you can count on my vote to: **
1. Raise the federal minimum wage to a living wage of at least $15
2. Pass the PRO-ACT which restores the right of workers to freely and fairly form a union and protects the right to collective bargaining
3. Support legislation that strengthens union rights in California and across the country.

[**I will be a champion for workers and unions when I’m elected to Congress. Now I need your help to get there. Can you chip in to send me to Washington, D.C.?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313302/304674786/-244427007?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhM2FjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nEjkzRm57dYlE5WGuYEfd-WLhLUG6bjbT0ift0pSwuY=)

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313307/304674793/-244427007?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhM2FjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nEjkzRm57dYlE5WGuYEfd-WLhLUG6bjbT0ift0pSwuY=)
Labor day is also a time to reflect on how the working people of this country are doing and where we want to be in the future. **The pandemic has shown us that unions are a worker’s first line of defense against unsafe and exploitative working conditions. **

Over the last 30 years the top 1% have seen a $22 trillion increase in their wealth, while the bottom half of the American people have actually seen a decline in their wealth. **We need an economy that works for all, not just the few.** We need leaders who will fight to raise the federal minimum wage to $15/hr and ensure that every worker has the opportunity to join a union.

While corporations and billionaires continue to grow their obscene wealth, tens of millions of workers have lost their jobs. Now, many families are struggling to put food on the table and avoid eviction. **Congress must act to protect workers and protect unions.** That’s why it’s absolutely crucial that we elect more progressive champions to Congress to advocate for working people.
**[With your donation, we can build the war chest we need to win this district and send another progressive champion to Congress.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313309/304674796/-244427007?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhM2FjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nEjkzRm57dYlE5WGuYEfd-WLhLUG6bjbT0ift0pSwuY=)**

Can I count on you?

In Solidarity,

**Derek Marshall**

name: Charlie Crist
subject: Celebrating the backbone of our country: the American worker
disclaimer_text: Paid for and approved by Charlie Crist, Democrat for Governor
body:
On this Labor Day, I want to take a moment to recognize the backbone of our country: the American worker. |
---

| | |
---
Friend,

On this Labor Day, I want to take a moment to recognize the backbone of our country: the American worker.

I want to especially celebrate our teachers, both in Florida and across the United States. They’ve endured incredible hardship to provide a future for our children, despite a raging pandemic.

This is personal for me -- two of my sisters were teachers. I have nothing but the utmost respect and appreciation for America’s educators, and I hope you’ll join me in thanking them -- and every worker -- for their contributions to this great nation.

Happy Labor Day!

\-- Charlie Crist

---

| Paid for and approved by Charlie Crist, Democrat for Governor
---
Charlie Crist for Governor
P.O. Box 1547
St. Petersburg, FL 33731
United States

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: Happy Labor Day, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Today we take time to honor the hardworking people who harnessed the power of collective bargaining to improve working conditions for people all across this country. Without the tireless efforts of the labor movement, we wouldn't have the 40-hour workweek, anti-discrimination laws, the minimum wage, Social Security, overtime pay, and so much more. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313419/304675004/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZGFjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fGy51G7uzsbAfievjsEJWzvjsdJ6wEbhylfWONWOwT0=&emci=5ec23c4b-d80c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=adac3d7d-2b0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=15820338)
---
Happy Labor Day, Peter!

Today we take time to honor the hardworking people who harnessed the power of collective bargaining to improve working conditions for people all across this country. Without the tireless efforts of the labor movement, we wouldn't have the 40-hour workweek, anti-discrimination laws, the minimum wage, Social Security, overtime pay, and so much more.

Growing up as the daughter of a unionized public school teacher, I saw the power of collective bargaining first-hand from my mother. As a child, I watched my mother join with her union to negotiate increased school funding, smaller class sizes, and improved benefits. Her union helped create not only better working conditions for my mother -- but better learning opportunities for her students as well.

I am so grateful for what the labor movement has accomplished for workers here in Washington State and across the country, but there is still work to be done. That's why I was proud to co-sponsor and help pass the PRO Act in the House earlier this year to protect the right to organize, and I'll continue to always fight for workers' rights.

**So today, please join me in honoring the accomplishments of the working people of the labor movement, and tomorrow, let's get back to work fighting alongside them for a better future.**

Thank you,

Kim

| Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress

name: Salud Carbajal
subject: This Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/V2G3DQ2blZADqO_nD1eX1ee21STLLKJw59lbu2TDuk8=/FWlJdqImsJMHCNYh8rjYFca9_B-lsnNeKxZ3xI03Yxc=)
Today, as we celebrate the achievements of the American worker, I hope you will join me in recognizing the many contributions made by millions of workers every day in order to strengthen our economy and our country.
Labor Day was established in the late nineteenth century at the urging of labor activists in order to recognize the great impact American workers have on our society.
As I celebrate this Labor Day with friends and local labor communities throughout the Central Coast, I am reminded of the rights and privileges we enjoy today because of the many labor leaders who made them possible.
I hope you will join me in acknowledging our local labor force today, and are able to celebrate this holiday safely and responsibly with friends and loved ones.
Best wishes for a safe and happy Labor Day!
Sincerely,
Salud Carbajal

Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313450/304675236/-2037180865?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MjcwOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWY4NDU5Ny0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=t36mFPOrUMd4D8HhVFHVe0E6AamV6dpUtYaJwCISUEA=&emci=4b848e73-e40c-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=aef84597-2b0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=12898431)
---
Friend,
As we enjoy the long weekend, I want to take a moment to celebrate the workers who built this country.
Thousands of Americans have marched, protested, and participated in strikes in order to create fairer, more equitable labor laws and safer workplaces -- _and still do to this day._
I am thankful for the working people who power Southern Arizona and for national victories such as child labor laws and family leave.
**I’m proud to stand with organized labor. In Congress, I will continue to advocate for sustainable clean energy jobs and bold investments in infrastructure as we emerge from the pandemic.**
Wishing you a safe and happy Labor Day,
Kirsten
---

name: Christy Smith
subject: A Labor Day message
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress Christy Smith for Congress
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313459/304675248/-1614289773?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjVkMzk3Ny0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qG-m3FvH3JNd1Gnf-Usnw91mczlKdqsLDQ6cBrQOtTA=&emci=844908d5-fc0b-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&emdi=465d3977-2b0f-ec11-981f-501ac57ba3ed&ceid=11385749)

Peter,

It's Labor Day -- and today is always about celebrating union workers in the Smith house. A reminder of gratitude we owe them for their service and sacrifice.

**I'm a proud former union member and leader.** When I went to community college, I worked on campus to help pay for my tuition. I joined the classified employees' union -- California Schools Employee Association Chapter 725 -- later becoming president. I even negotiated our contract when I was still in my 20s.

**Working folks have always delivered for us** \-- and now, they deserve a government that will fight for them during the greatest economic crisis in a century. The best way to improve the quality of life and vibrancy of our communities is to provide a supportive and equitable environment for job and career creation.

With so many working families struggling to make ends meet, it has been made abundantly clear that building an economy that works for everyone must be a priority, even as we continue to fight COVID-19. Unions are leading this fight even to this day.

**Everyone deserves a job that pays a living wage. Everyone deserves a secure retirement. Everyone deserves a workplace that is safe. And we'll never stop fighting until that's a reality.**

Happy Labor Day!

\-- Christy [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313460/304675249/-392799195?refcode=em21_210906_laborday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjVkMzk3Ny0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qG-m3FvH3JNd1Gnf-Usnw91mczlKdqsLDQ6cBrQOtTA=)

name: Karen Bass
subject: Respecting and protecting our workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Karen Bass for Congress
body:
Team --

While some of us enjoy the day off today, we can't forget why we're celebrating Labor Day.

It's no exaggeration to say that America wouldn't be what it is today without labor unions. Today, we recognize the sacrifice of the organizers and workers who helped secure revolutionary victories for working people, including the five-day work-week, better wages, and safer working conditions.

I want to thank our nurses, teachers, sanitation workers, postal workers, health care workers, and more who are risking their own health to serve our communities. And I want to recognize the unions that help build power for working people. I am standing in solidarity with the countless frontline workers who are fighting for better pay and working conditions -- especially as cases of COVID-19 continue to rise.

Right now, unions are fighting for hazard pay, proper personal protective equipment, paid sick leave, and more, for America's workers. I'm a proud co-sponsor of the Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act, and I support raising the minimum wage to $15. But we can't stop there, friend.

**We are leaning on our essential workers more than ever before, and it 's crucial we support the leaders and policies that put working people first. Today, and every day, I hope you'll join me in respecting, protecting, and supporting America's workers.**

In solidarity,

Karen

Karen Bass started as a community organizer, so she knows just how much a $5 or $10 donation can do. Help fuel her movement to make social and economic justice for all a reality by chipping in today.

name: Nancy Pelosi
subject: I know you're busy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
---
| | | [_**I know I don’t normally send you emails this long -- especially over your long weekend. But I felt that you needed to know. If you have a minute, it would mean everything to me if you read this entire email. It’s critical that I explain to you what’s going on behind the scenes in our fight for the House. Then, can I count on your $15? >>**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313646/304675968/1694321165?refcode=20210906_em_fr_b1_pelosi_30_rs&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5OWFjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bMMCVop-Wfw8kWcJD4O48EBxHfTS0iTP5ZLBsjTyKFM=)
* * *
**[_NBC NEWS: "EARLY INDICATORS SUGGEST DEMOCRATS’ HOUSE MAJORITY IS IN JEOPARDY"_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35313647/304675969/1694321165?refcode=20210906_em_fr_b1_pelosi_30_rs&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5OWFjM2Q3ZC0yYjBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bMMCVop-Wfw8kWcJD4O48EBxHfTS0iTP5ZLBsjTyKFM=)** Republicans just launched an enormous effort to take away the House from us. body:
Let’s celebrate the American Labor Movement and all the workers who keep our country running.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/35314085/304678702/-182076786?refcode=BLU-SJC-20210906-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTMxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOGE0ZTdlMi0yYzBmLWVjMTEtOTgxZi01MDFhYzU3YmEzZWQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hodVevd4h0Dn_wq9OqrAbtFF81ofGuCEaeVmUdXRe2E=)

| I know a lot of folks are taking this long weekend to spend some much-needed time outside, and I hope you are able to safely celebrate with your loved ones. Especially as we near the end of another difficult summer, it's more important than ever that we take some time away to recharge.

So today, let’s celebrate the American Labor Movement and all the workers who keep our country running, even in the middle of a pandemic. And let’s remember why we are able to take time away, because of the tireless work of the American Labor Movement.

This Labor Day, we must commit to not only celebrating all the incredible workers who keep our country running, but to investing in them as well. I am proud to be a champion in Congress for policies like paid leave and affordable child care that support our workers.

Happy Labor Day! Wear a mask, get vaccinated, practice physical distancing, and be safe!

Warmly,

Sara
---

name: Stewart Navarre
subject: What today means to me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY STEWART NAVARRE FOR CONGRESS
body:
Peter,
I want to take a moment today to wish you and your family a Happy Labor Day!
On this day we celebrate the key to our economy and our daily lives - the American worker - and all we have accomplished together to improve safety, working conditions, fair pay, and eliminate child labor. The American union movement has made the difference for all of us in ensuring the worker has a voice in America and a seat at the table.
Few things are more important than ensuring that all working Virginians have a fair shot. I will be a champion of workers in Congress and I look forward to having the opportunity to prove it.
I wish you and your family good health and a Happy Labor Day!
-- Stewart
Stewart Navarre is a Democrat running for Virginia's 1st Congressional District. He's ready to serve his fellow Virginians with integrity, ethics, and hard work. Support Stewart with a donation today.

subject: This Labor Day

body:
# [#]
Peter,

I wanted to take a moment to wish you a Happy Labor Day.

As we continue to face the ramifications of the pandemic, we’re more thankful than ever for our essential workers, medical personnel, and all those who helped our country get through these past few years.

I continue to be committed to fighting for working families here in California and nationwide. I hope you’ll join me this weekend in reflecting on the labor movement, how far we’ve come, and how far we still have to go.

Thank you, and Happy Labor Day,

Mike Thompson

name: =?utf-8?Q?Douglas=20Carswell?=
subject: Mississippi Economic Bulletin - September
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/economicbulletinsept?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| **Unemployment: Still Stubbornly High**

New Bureau of Labor data shows that unemployment in Mississippi remained stubbornly high in July at 6.1 percent – little changed from what it was over the previous quarter.

Unemployment in Mississippi peaked at 15.7 percent in April 2020, at the height of the Covid shutdown. But as this latest data confirms, unemployment still remains significantly above what it was before the pandemic, when the unemployment rate was as low as 4.8 percent.
---
|
---
| This new data shows why we need to make it easier for people to work and for businesses to hire. That means removing occupational licensing and abolishing the state income tax, which is a tax on payroll.

Mississippi’s jobless rate, according to this latest data, is above that in neighboring states with low or no state income tax, such as Tennessee (4.7 percent) and Alabama (3.2 percent).

Despite a labor shortage nationally and in Mississippi, the number of those without work is above what it was before the pandemic. This suggests that some of the federal Covid relief measures might have contributed to the labor shortage by creating unintended incentives.

**Fiscal: State Budget Surplus**

Data shows that Mississippi’s state budget is in record surplus. Total revenue collections for FY 2021 as of August 1st, 2021 were $6,741,012,015, meaning that the budget has a surplus of about $1,164 million – according to new data released by the Joint Legislative Budget Committee.

Much of the budget surplus is illusory, however. It is a consequence of federal handouts and Covid spending. A significant portion of the $1 billion surplus, however, is expected to be recurring. This means that Mississippi’s fiscal status offers policymakers a historic opportunity to offer meaningful tax breaks without incurring debt or spending any ‘rainy day’ funds.

To receive monthly Mississippi Economic Bulletin updates, sign up at [www.MsPolicy.org](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=31e620be19&e=5703353baa)

clean_body: | | | | | | Unemployment: Still Stubbornly High New Bureau of Labor data shows that unemployment in Mississippi remained stubbornly high in July at 6.1 percent – little changed from what it was over the previous quarter. Unemployment in Mississippi peaked at 15.7 percent in April 2020, at the height of the Covid shutdown. But as this latest data confirms, unemployment still remains significantly above what it was before the pandemic, when the unemployment rate was as low as 4.8 percent. | | This new data shows why we need to make it easier for people to work and for businesses to hire. That means removing occupational licensing and abolishing the state income tax, which is a tax on payroll. Mississippi’s jobless rate, according to this latest data, is above that in neighboring states with low or no state income tax, such as Tennessee (4.7 percent) and Alabama (3.2 percent). Despite a labor shortage nationally and in Mississippi, the number of those without work is above what it was before the pandemic. This suggests that some of the federal Covid relief measures might have contributed to the labor shortage by creating unintended incentives. Fiscal: State Budget Surplus Data shows that Mississippi’s state budget is in record surplus. Total revenue collections for FY 2021 as of August 1st, 2021 were $6,741,012,015, meaning that the budget has a surplus of about $1,164 million – according to new data released by the Joint Legislative Budget Committee. Much of the budget surplus is illusory, however. It is a consequence of federal handouts and Covid spending. A significant portion of the $1 billion surplus, however, is expected to be recurring. This means that Mississippi’s fiscal status offers policymakers a historic opportunity to offer meaningful tax breaks without incurring debt or spending any ‘rainy day’ funds. name: Mike Collier
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Collier Committee
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/89772/images/Logo%20%282%29.png]
Happy Labor Day, folks!

Today and every day, we honor the legacy of American workers who built the Lone Star State and the sacrifices they made in service to the next generation.

As we reflect upon their contributions, I’m reminded of my father and grandfather, who were both proud trade union members. Unions gave them the opportunity to reach the middle class and provide a life of dignity and prosperity for their family.

It’s because of their commitment to organizing for fair wages, better health care, and safe working conditions that the American Dream was possible for so many folks.

To all the doctors, nurses, emergency responders, and hospital workers working overtime to make sure our communities are safe and healthy — thank you.

To all the grocery workers, delivery drivers, postal workers, and essential workers holding our nation together during a pandemic — thank you.

To all the teachers, librarians, support staff, and bus drivers working every day to inspire our children — thank you.

We owe a lifetime of gratitude to the millions of working Texans who are the lifeblood of our great state. There’s still a lot of work we have left to do to empower our workers and unions to build better, safer, and more equitable conditions for all Texans.

That is why we must stand together to fight for an economy that works for Texas families — not just corporations and lobbyists.

Stay safe and enjoy y’all,

— Mike Collier

name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Protecting American workers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marcy Kaptur for Congress
body:
On this Labor Day, I’d like to recognize the sacrifices of those who built the labor movement. |
Marcy Kaptur | |
---
[](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xNXS5KvDi5wrxDE5VytOtCwVpc5i8Die8BmDyLxt2uxwlzw1rN1mHC1ks8RlIbi5ZuSlNrUOkpZYsFCuC39ru5frt0H4SpC2jS-ZXKr7ohTb4p6sN5DHA0WV0Anz2GSkaUPnJGvFQHKE3hdmv8T-GODNbzC3kgJmKUF1ctfwTjWxDF7HUtbLfrpCGwdrsIUt7n9fMTp3kBxOjje17ASjbo1lpf3iD2swYzKXqNCcD9mgFqfV56B4vNMCgLbGCb-3pjY7fuXXtnEEQhjUrjOZI8QG8K4-04zjSPwB6s90Pw-80UVQ-QSs1_yIzIe0o5V9HuXFNfIF3Q51alV2IzuRO04DGgAhuMmdOGSeDSEQXf3w/3f3/SPLMfe-cTc-N6VHhGpZ_1Q/h0/M078N-ZpWWdLATs1xxuYr2TiNsO2Po-47JyPZQL92E4)

| | Team,

On this Labor Day, we recognize the sacrifices of those who built the labor movement and, in so doing, achieved important advancements such as employer-based health care, worker safety protections, the 40-hour workweek, and fair pay for overtime. The American labor movement makes life better for us all, and so we honor hard-fought progress this Labor Day.

As a fierce advocate for the **protection and expansion of labor rights,** I deeply value our nurses, teachers, skilled tradespeople, labor unions, firefighters, law enforcement, and working-class families.

**We are all _enriched_ by the contributions and achievements of the men and women who make our communities and our economy work.**

Protecting American workers and strengthening their ability to organize in support of their collective interests and goals is more urgent than ever. We must renew our promise to the American worker and advocate for policies that respect the dignity of their work.

Thank you for your perseverance and commitment to serving our community. I wish you and your family a happy and relaxing Labor Day.

Stay safe,

Marcy Kaptur

| Congresswoman Marcy Kaptur is a champion for the Midwest in Congress. She is focused on getting big money out of politics, holding Wall Street accountable, fighting against bad trade deals that harm workers, ensuring access to health care for all, and restoring our Great Lakes. **Can you help us send Marcy Kaptur back to Congress?**

name: Tom Nelson
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin**
body:
---
| | Listen,

I hope you are enjoying a well-earned day off today. As you probably know, Labor Day is a holiday to honor and recognize the American labor movement and its achievements.

Weekends, paid-time-off, health care, eight-hour workdays, and many more tangible, everyday benefits were made possible by strikes and unionizing of workers generations before us.

While this is a day to celebrate the contributions of past generations, it’s also a day to look forward and take stock of what still needs to be done.

Union workers across our state continue to be exploited and denied dignity. Earlier this spring, I visited workers in Janesville that are currently fighting for their livelihoods because a private equity firm decided to move 200 jobs from Janesville to Mexico.

What’s happening in Janesville has and continues to occur across our state. We need elected officials that will side with workers every time.

**That’s why I’m proudly pro-worker and pro-union._As you know, I even wrote a book about successfully standing up to Wall Street and saving a paper mill._**

That’s why I’m incredibly proud to have Union endorsements that include:
* Green Bay Area Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 141
* Appleton Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 257
* Grand Chute Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 3655
* OPCMIA Local 599 (Operative Plasterers' and Cement Masons' International Association)
* Dave Branson, Local labor leader
* Kilah Engelke, Secretary Treasurer, Milwaukee Building-Construction Trades Council
* Jon Geenen, former United Steelworkers International Vice-President
* Tim Jacobson, Chief steward, UAW Local 578
* John Matthews, Former Executive Director of Madison Teachers Inc.
* Nick Liesch, Sheet Metal Workers Local 18, business representative (retired), Past President of NorthEast Wisconsin Building and Construction Trades Council
* Ryan Steffel, President, IAFF Local 1594 (Kaukauna)
* Joe Resch, Vice-President, IAFF Local 1594 (Kaukauna)

On Labor Day, I want to let you know where I stand; **that will always be on the side of hard-working Wisconsinites.**

Happy Labor Day,

Tom Nelson

clean_body: | | Listen, I hope you are enjoying a well-earned day off today. As you probably know, Labor Day is a holiday to honor and recognize the American labor movement and its achievements. Weekends, paid-time-off, health care, eight-hour workdays, and many more tangible, everyday benefits were made possible by strikes and unionizing of workers generations before us. While this is a day to celebrate the contributions of past generations, it’s also a day to look forward and take stock of what still needs to be done. Union workers across our state continue to be exploited and denied dignity. Earlier this spring, I visited workers in Janesville that are currently fighting for their livelihoods because a private equity firm decided to move 200 jobs from Janesville to Mexico. What’s happening in Janesville has and continues to occur across our state. We need elected officials that will side with workers every time. That’s why I’m proudly pro-worker and pro-union.As you know, I even wrote a book about successfully standing up to Wall Street and saving a paper mill. That’s why I’m incredibly proud to have Union endorsements that include: Green Bay Area Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 141 Appleton Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 257 Grand Chute Fire Fighters I.A.F.F. Local 3655 OPCMIA Local 599 (Operative Plasterers' and Cement Masons' International Association) Dave Branson, Local labor leader Kilah Engelke, Secretary Treasurer, Milwaukee Building-Construction Trades Council Jon Geenen, former United Steelworkers International Vice-President Tim Jacobson, Chief steward, UAW Local 578 John Matthews, Former Executive Director of Madison Teachers Inc. Nick Liesch, Sheet Metal Workers Local 18, business representative (retired), Past President of NorthEast Wisconsin Building and Construction Trades Council Ryan Steffel, President, IAFF Local 1594 (Kaukauna) Joe Resch, Vice-President, IAFF Local 1594 (Kaukauna) On Labor Day, I want to let you know where I stand; that will always be on the side of hard-working Wisconsinites. Happy Labor Day, Tom Nelson | | | | = Tom Nelson serves as County Executive for Outagamie, one of the worst-hit COVID areas in the nation. Local officials have been leading our country through the pandemic because Donald Trump and Ron Johnson have failed. Tom is running for U.S. Senate because we need real leaders in Washington who will take COVID seriously. And if we can take on a pandemic, we can take on anything. And if you can win in a red county six times, you can win anywhere. If you’d like to unsubscribe, = Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin Nelson for Wisconsin PO Box 361 Kaukauna, WI 54130 United States**

name: Josh Weil for US Senate
subject: Josh Weil for Senate - Newsletter
body:
Issue: 006 Monday, September 6, 2021 | First and foremost, Happy Labor Day! Today is a day we overwhelmingly take for granted, because it is
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/feNkxHRRh/c?w=FirkE87yhxqnWvmWSovKGDVt8Ls45KIHF-UQURdueFw.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zaG91dG91dC53aXguY29tL3NvL2ZlTmt4SFJSaD9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6Ijg5ZmI1NTQ5LTQ5MzQtNGM0OS1iZDRmLTRhZWEyZTg2MzgxNiJ9)_**
---
| | |
---
| | Josh Weil for Senate - Newsletter
---
| |
---
| | Issue: 006 Monday, September 6, 2021
---
|
---
| | First and foremost, Happy Labor Day! Today is a day we overwhelmingly take for granted, because it is meant to be a reprieve, a day of rest, and a three day weekend for those who work to live. But, it is also so much more than that, a memorial to the activists who fought and sometimes died to unionize and create a standard of rights for workers in the Western World that has become so normalized, we have trouble even imagining the way it used to be. Let's not forget that public schools are a byproduct of the victories of the labor movement, an inevitable concession, once child labor was relegated to the dustbins of history. Yesterday's labor champions had to withstand violent opposition to their strikes and protests, imprisonment and hunger strikes, and threats to their neighbors and families to gain collective bargaining rights, a 40 hour work week, safe workplaces, and THE WEEKEND! These changes were all necessary as the model for managing an agrarian world did not perfectly translate to the new industrial one they lived in. Similarly, today, we must continue to support our Union and Labor leaders as they fight tirelessly against those that want to superimpose the traditions and norms of an industrial world onto this new digital one. If you're not already a member of a Union, find one to support. Union leaders fight for all of us, whether you're a member or not, and we'd be dead without them. Happy Labor Day!
---
| | [](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/feNkxHRRh/c?w=Wj6aXpObSupdHND-iq8G9Y4xjnSBUXdTbHKYPWu63rA.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZmFjZWJvb2suY29tL2V2ZW50cy80MDAyNDk4OTc5MzE4NDQiLCJyIjoiOGQ5NjBmNGYtZmYwMy00YjE0LTQzYzYtYjZhYjJjNWMyYTgyIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiODlmYjU1NDktNDkzNC00YzQ5LWJkNGYtNGFlYTJlODYzODE2In0)
---
| | IN REVIEW It's been a month since the Endless Summer finally ended, and I reported back to work for the 2021-22 school year. As a teacher and a political candidate, the intensity has been cranked up to 11, with the intersection of my personal Venn Diagram becoming a dangerous political hotbed. Our Governor tried making it illegal for local governments to go into lockdown or for schools to order mask mandates, and with Wave 4 of COVID and the Delta Variant decimating our state, he doubled down by authorizing the Commissioner of Education to begin withholding funds from school districts that defied him in order to protect their teachers and children. It has been a long month, with a lot of school board meetings, and a tremendous amount of politics forcing our district leaders into unenviable positions. Whatever county you are in, please, if you can, attend your local school board meetings and make your voice heard. I'll be at mine, again, tomorrow night. Inside the campaign, I want to announce the addition of Rocco Diaz to our team. He has joined the campaign in a part-time, hourly position as my new Call-Time Manager, and like all of our hourly workers, is paid $15/hr. I strongly believe in transparency when it comes to salaries and compensation, we will never hide what the employees at Weil for US Senate make, and we will never pay less than $15/hr. In order to pay people what they're worth, we have to have money, though. [Please, if you can, consider making a contribution today. And if you are with us for the long haul, consider making it a recurring monthly contribution.](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/feNkxHRRh/c?w=epyheItQI_Yfhp4wRv4ALxuPZNklCBF_vLjn2xZZwXw.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL3dlaWxqZmxzZW5hdGUiLCJyIjoiZWJlZmVhYzItYmZhNS00Y2QzLTQwOTctYjMzYzc2YTAxMzUzIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiODlmYjU1NDktNDkzNC00YzQ5LWJkNGYtNGFlYTJlODYzODE2In0)
---
| | [](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/feNkxHRRh/c?w=nt0rvOSyickQHy5VFipBNaO88YlrlitdQVryS-_3FJk.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zaG91dG91dC53aXguY29tL3NvL2ZlTmt4SFJSaD9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsInIiOiI2ZWI0MzA1ZS1hZDdmLTQ1NTEtZjA1Yy02MGQyMjVmMzVhYjgiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI4OWZiNTU0OS00OTM0LTRjNDktYmQ0Zi00YWVhMmU4NjM4MTYifQ)
---
|
---
| | WHAT'S NEXT I am back on the teacher schedule, so I can't travel as much as I did this summer, but we are still getting around. Already, this month, I've made canvassing trips to Bradford and Broward County, and we have additional trips coming up to Immokalee, Jacksonville, Fernandina Beach, Miami, and Tallahassee, all before Green Day wakes up. Thank you for your continued support, we've crossed the threshold, and now have less than a year remaining to the Primary Elections on August 23rd. It's a long road, getting from there to here, but we're going to walk every step of it and I can't wait to meet you all along the way! Together, we can win, and I can't wait to serve YOU as your next U.S. Senator! Stay Safe! -Josh
---
| | | [**DONATE TO SUPPORT THE CAMPAIGN**](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/feNkxHRRh/c?w=tBjQd0v3CK8iSAnBlh-85TWxa5hFMnPr6bpmtReqCj8.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL3dlaWxqZmxzZW5hdGUiLCJyIjoiN2M4ZTAwOTctMGQyNC00ZDBlLWFmYzgtMDk5Y2UzNjgxZDVlIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiODlmYjU1NDktNDkzNC00YzQ5LWJkNGYtNGFlYTJlODYzODE2In0)
